faqs.a: Grounding techniques are coping skills that use the senses and surroundings to bring a person back to the present moment. They help interrupt anxiety, panic, flashbacks, and dissociation by giving the mind something concrete to hold onto right now.

It's a sensory grounding technique where you name five things you can see, four you can hear, three you can touch, two you can smell, and one you can taste. It directs attention to present-moment sensations to interrupt distress.

No. Grounding is a coping tool for acute moments, not a treatment for the underlying anxiety, trauma, or condition driving the distress. It manages symptoms in the moment rather than resolving root causes, so it works best alongside proper care.
